title: SQLite foreign keys not enabled: REFERENCES constraints silently ignored
description: db.rs creates the connection pool with SqliteConnectOptions but does not call .foreign_keys(true) (or run PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON). SQLite defaults to foreign_keys = OFF. This means the REFERENCES constraints in the schema (e.g. delivery_queue.post_id REFERENCES posts(id), followers.persona_id REFERENCES personas(id)) are not enforced at runtime. Orphaned delivery queue rows for deleted posts, or followers referencing non-existent personas, can accumulate silently. Fix: add .foreign_keys(true) to the SqliteConnectOptions builder in db::connect.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
